source_clause_title: 23.3. ACCEPTABILITY OF INSURERS:  Insurance is to be placed with insurers with a current

entities.name: A.M. Best’s rating of no less than A: VII
entities.legal_role: Specifies the minimum financial strength and reliability rating required for insurers providing coverage under the contract, ensuring insurer acceptability and risk mitigation for the District.
entities.evidence_quote: A.M. Best’s rating of no less than A: VII, unless otherwise acceptable to the District.

entities.name: District
entities.legal_role: The public entity contracting for services, with rights to approve insurers, occupy sites, and limit liability under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: unless otherwise acceptable to the District.

entities.name: Contractor
entities.legal_role: The party responsible for performing the work, guaranteeing workmanship and materials, complying with laws, and subject to warranty and labor code obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: the Contractor, manufacturer, or their assigned agents shall guarantee the workmanship, product or service performed...

entities.name: minimum period of one (1) year from filing the Notice of Completion
entities.legal_role: Defines the minimum time period during which the Contractor guarantees workmanship and materials against defects, establishing the timeframe for warranty claims.
entities.evidence_quote: for a minimum period of one (1) year from filing the Notice of Completion with the county in which the Site is located.

entities.name: Notice of Completion
entities.legal_role: The official filing that triggers the start of the warranty period for workmanship and materials.
entities.evidence_quote: from filing the Notice of Completion with the county in which the Site is located.

entities.name: all information, documents, programs, procedures, and all other items received from the other Party that is marked or otherwise designated as confidential
entities.legal_role: Information protected under the confidentiality obligations of the Parties, subject to survival beyond termination and exceptions for legal compliance.
entities.evidence_quote: Each Party shall maintain the confidentiality of all information, documents, programs, procedures, and all other items received from the other Party that is marked or otherwise designated as confidential.

entities.name: Each Party
entities.legal_role: The entities bound by confidentiality, compliance, and other contractual obligations under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Each Party shall maintain the confidentiality...

entities.name: California Public Records Act and Brown Act open meeting requirements
entities.legal_role: Legal statutes that impose transparency and disclosure obligations on the District, affecting confidentiality and information disclosure under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: The Parties understand that the District is subject to compliance with the California Public Records Act and Brown Act open meeting requirements...

entities.name: District’s financial obligations under this Agreement shall be limited to the payment of the compensation provided in this Agreement
entities.legal_role: Caps the District’s financial exposure to the contract price and excludes liability for consequential damages, protecting the District from extended financial risk.
entities.evidence_quote: District’s financial obligations under this Agreement shall be limited to the payment of the compensation provided in this Agreement.

entities.name: Contractor shall give all notices and comply with all laws, ordinance, rules and regulations bearing on conduct of the Work
entities.legal_role: Mandates the Contractor’s duty to comply with applicable laws and notify the District of any legal conflicts, enabling contract amendments or termination.
entities.evidence_quote: Contractor shall give all notices and comply with all laws, ordinance, rules and regulations bearing on conduct of the Work as indicated or specified.

entities.name: this Agreement shall be terminated effective upon Contractor’s receipt of a written termination notice from the District
entities.legal_role: Grants the District the right to terminate the Agreement if the Contractor fails to comply with laws and does not notify the District of violations.
entities.evidence_quote: this Agreement shall be terminated effective upon Contractor’s receipt of a written termination notice from the District.

entities.name: The Contractor shall comply with all applicable provisions of the California Labor Code, Division 3, Part 7, Chapter 1, Articles 1 – 5
entities.legal_role: Requires the Contractor and subcontractors to comply with wage, discrimination, and apprenticeship laws, with penalties for willful noncompliance.
entities.evidence_quote: The Contractor shall comply with all applicable provisions of the California Labor Code, Division 3, Part 7, Chapter 1, Articles 1 – 5...

relationships.legal_significance: The District reserves the right to accept insurers with ratings lower than A: VII, giving it control over insurer selection and risk exposure.
relationships.evidence_quote: A.M. Best’s rating of no less than A: VII, unless otherwise acceptable to the District.

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor is legally obligated to guarantee workmanship and materials for at least one year from the Notice of Completion, defining the warranty period.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Contractor... shall guarantee the workmanship, product or service performed against defective workmanship, defects or failures of materials for a minimum period of one (1) year from filing the Notice of Completion...

relationships.legal_significance: The filing of the Notice of Completion triggers the start of the warranty period, establishing a clear temporal boundary for warranty claims.
relationships.evidence_quote: for a minimum period of one (1) year from filing the Notice of Completion with the county in which the Site is located.

relationships.legal_significance: Each Party is legally bound to maintain the confidentiality of designated information, protecting sensitive data exchanged under the Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: Each Party shall maintain the confidentiality of all information, documents, programs, procedures, and all other items received from the other Party that is marked or otherwise designated as confidential.

relationships.legal_significance: The District must comply with public records and open meeting laws, which may limit confidentiality and require disclosure of information.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Parties understand that the District is subject to compliance with the California Public Records Act and Brown Act open meeting requirements...

relationships.legal_significance: Limits the District’s financial liability strictly to contract compensation, excluding consequential damages, thereby reducing financial risk.
relationships.evidence_quote: District’s financial obligations under this Agreement shall be limited to the payment of the compensation provided in this Agreement.

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor must comply with all applicable laws and notify the District of any violations, ensuring lawful performance of work.
relationships.evidence_quote: Contractor shall give all notices and comply with all laws, ordinance, rules and regulations bearing on conduct of the Work...

relationships.legal_significance: Allows the District to terminate the Agreement if the Contractor violates laws and fails to notify the District, protecting the District from unlawful work.
relationships.evidence_quote: this Agreement shall be terminated effective upon Contractor’s receipt of a written termination notice from the District.

relationships.legal_significance: Imposes on the Contractor and subcontractors the duty to comply with California labor laws, including wage and anti-discrimination provisions, with penalties for noncompliance.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Contractor shall comply with all applicable provisions of the California Labor Code...

extractor_notes: The clause text includes multiple distinct provisions beyond insurer acceptability, covering warranty, confidentiality, liability limitation, compliance with laws, termination rights, and labor code compliance, reflecting a composite of contract obligations rather than a single focused clause.

entities.name: Contractor
entities.legal_role: The party responsible for performing the Work and liable for compliance with laws, proper disposal of waste, and indemnification obligations under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: If the Contractor fails to comply with any applicable laws, rules, or regulations... the Contractor will be held solely responsible...

entities.name: District
entities.legal_role: The party entitled to indemnification and protection from damages and claims arising from the Contractor's failure to comply with laws or contamination events.
entities.evidence_quote: shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the District, pursuant to the indemnification provisions of the Agreement...

entities.name: Indemnification provisions
entities.legal_role: Contractual provisions that govern the Contractor's obligation to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the District from damages and claims.
entities.evidence_quote: shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the District, pursuant to the indemnification provisions of the Agreement...

entities.name: Work involving lead disturbance
entities.legal_role: Specific scope of Work requiring persons with appropriate accreditation, registrations, licenses, and training to perform it.
entities.evidence_quote: If lead disturbance is anticipated in the Work, only persons with appropriate accreditation, registrations, licenses, and training shall conduct this Work.

entities.name: Certified hazardous waste hauler
entities.legal_role: Entity responsible for proper disposal of hazardous waste, required to be certified and registered with DOT, and able to issue a current manifest number.
entities.evidence_quote: It will be the responsibility of the Contractor to provide the proper disposal of any hazardous waste by a certified hazardous waste hauler. This company shall be registered with the Department of Transportation (DOT) and shall be able to issue a current manifest number...

entities.name: Sample results
entities.legal_role: Data or reports that the Contractor must provide to the District before, during, and after the Work to demonstrate compliance or conditions.
entities.evidence_quote: The Contractor shall provide the District with any sample results prior to beginning Work, during the Work, and after the completion of the Work.

entities.name: Lead training records
entities.legal_role: Records of employee training related to lead disturbance that the District may request to examine prior to Work commencement.
entities.evidence_quote: The District may request to examine, prior to the commencement of the Work, the lead training records of each employee of the Contractor.

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor is legally obligated to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the District for damages and claims arising from the Contractor's failure to comply with laws causing contamination.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Contractor will be held solely responsible for all costs involved in any required corrective actions, and shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the District...

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor's indemnification duties are governed by the indemnification provisions of the Agreement, defining the scope and manner of indemnity.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the District, pursuant to the indemnification provisions of the Agreement...

relationships.legal_significance: Only Contractor personnel with appropriate accreditation, registrations, licenses, and training may perform Work involving lead disturbance, limiting liability and ensuring compliance.
relationships.evidence_quote: only persons with appropriate accreditation, registrations, licenses, and training shall conduct this Work.

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor must ensure hazardous waste is disposed of by a certified hazardous waste hauler registered with DOT, ensuring legal compliance in waste transport and disposal.
relationships.evidence_quote: It will be the responsibility of the Contractor to provide the proper disposal of any hazardous waste by a certified hazardous waste hauler. This company shall be registered with the Department of Transportation (DOT)...

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor must provide sample results to the District before, during, and after the Work, enabling the District to monitor compliance and site conditions.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Contractor shall provide the District with any sample results prior to beginning Work, during the Work, and after the completion of the Work.

relationships.legal_significance: The District has the right to request and examine the lead training records of each Contractor employee prior to Work commencement to verify qualifications and compliance.
relationships.evidence_quote: The District may request to examine, prior to the commencement of the Work, the lead training records of each employee of the Contractor.

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly defines the Contractor's liability for contamination and regulatory non-compliance, including indemnification obligations, qualifications for lead disturbance work, hazardous waste disposal requirements, and reporting duties to the District. The indemnification relationship is explicitly tied to the Agreement's indemnification provisions.

source_clause_title: 6. Materials charges will be billed according to the Contractor list price in effect at the

entities.name: Omnidian Inc.
entities.legal_role: The party responsible for performing the Work under the Agreement and complying with all labor, safety, and certification requirements.
entities.evidence_quote: Proper Name of Contractor: Omnidian Inc.

entities.name: District
entities.legal_role: The contracting public entity that oversees the Project and enforces compliance with contract terms and legal requirements.
entities.evidence_quote: The undersigned does hereby certify to the District that I am a representative of the Contractor currently under contract with the District

entities.entity_type: State of California Public Works Contract Requirements

entities.legal_role: Statutory and regulatory labor standards that the Contractor must comply with for the Project.
entities.evidence_quote: I hereby certify that I will conform to the State of California Public Works Contract requirements regarding prevailing wages, benefits, on -site audits with 48 -hours’ notice, payroll records, and apprentice and trainee employment requirements

entities.name: Labor Code section 3700
entities.legal_role: Statutory provision requiring employers to secure workers' compensation insurance or self-insure before commencing work.
entities.evidence_quote: Labor Code section 3700 in relevant part provides: Every employer except the State shall secure the payment of compensation in one or more of the following ways

entities.name: Workers’ Compensation Certification
entities.legal_role: Certification by the Contractor that it will comply with workers' compensation insurance or self-insurance requirements before starting work.
entities.evidence_quote: I am aware of the provisions of section 3700 of the Labor Code which require every employer to be insured against liability for workers' compensation or to undertake self -insurance in accordance with the provisions of that code, and I will comply with such provisions before commencing the performance of the Work of this Agreement.

entities.name: Education Code section 45125.2(a)
entities.legal_role: Statutory provision governing fingerprinting and criminal background checks for employees working on school sites.
entities.evidence_quote: Pursuant to Education Code section 45125.2(a), Contractor has installed or will install, prior to commencement of Work, a physical barrier at the Work Site

entities.name: Education Code section 45125.1(h)
entities.legal_role: Statutory provision requiring fingerprinting and DOJ submission for sole proprietors working on school projects.
entities.evidence_quote: If Contractor is a sole proprietor, and elects the above option, Contractor must have the above-named employee’s fingerprints prepared and submitted by District for submission to the DOJ, in accordance with Education Code section 45125.1(h).

entities.name: California Department of Justice
entities.legal_role: State agency responsible for ascertaining criminal background and fingerprint clearance for Contractor’s employees and subcontractors.
entities.evidence_quote: No work shall commence until such determination by DOJ has been made.

entities.name: Contractor’s employees and all of its Subcontractors’ employees who may have contact with District pupils
entities.legal_role: Individuals subject to fingerprinting and criminal background checks under Education Code requirements.
entities.evidence_quote: Contractor certifies that it has taken at least one of the following actions... The Contractor, who is not a sole proprietor, has complied with the fingerprinting requirements... with respect to all Contractor’s employees and all of its Subcontractors’ employees who may have contact with District pupils

entities.name: ATTACHMENT “A”
entities.legal_role: Document listing all employees and subcontractors who may come in contact with District pupils, used for compliance with background check requirements.
entities.evidence_quote: A complete and accurate list of Contractor's employees and of all of its subcontractors' employees who may come in contact with District pupils during the course and scope of the Contract is attached hereto as ATTACHMENT “A ;”

entities.name: Hazardous Materials Certification
entities.legal_role: Contractor’s certification that no asbestos, PCB, or other hazardous materials will be used or incorporated in the Project or related equipment.
entities.evidence_quote: Contractor hereby certifies that no asbestos, or asbestos- containing materials, polychlorinated biphenyl (PCB), or any material listed by the federal or state Environmental Protection Agency or federal or state health agencies as a hazardous material... shall be furnished, installed, or incorporated in any way into the Project

relationships.relationship_type: Obligation to Comply with Labor Requirements

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor certifies it will secure workers' compensation insurance or self-insure as required by law before starting work.
relationships.evidence_quote: I am aware of the provisions of section 3700 of the Labor Code... and I will comply with such provisions before commencing the performance of the Work of this Agreement.

relationships.relationship_type: Subject to DOJ Criminal Background Clearance
relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor’s employees and subcontractors must be cleared by the DOJ before commencing work to ensure no felony convictions as defined by Education Code.
relationships.evidence_quote: No work shall commence until the Department of Justice ascertains that Contractor’s employees and any subcontractors’ employees have not been convicted of a felony

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor must provide a complete and accurate list of employees and subcontractors who may contact District pupils to ensure compliance with background check requirements.
relationships.evidence_quote: A complete and accurate list of Contractor's employees and of all of its subcontractors' employees who may come in contact with District pupils during the course and scope of the Contract is attached hereto as ATTACHMENT “A ;”

relationships.relationship_type: Certification of Non-Use of Hazardous Materials
relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor certifies that no hazardous materials will be used or incorporated in the Project, ensuring compliance with environmental and safety laws.
relationships.evidence_quote: Contractor hereby certifies that no asbestos, or asbestos- containing materials, polychlorinated biphenyl (PCB), or any material listed by the federal or state Environmental Protection Agency or federal or state health agencies as a hazardous material... shall be furnished, installed, or incorporated in any way into the Project

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor is under contract with the District to perform the Work and comply with all contract terms and legal requirements.
relationships.evidence_quote: The undersigned does hereby certify to the District that I am a representative of the Contractor currently under contract with the District

extractor_notes: This clause bundle contains multiple certifications and declarations by the Contractor Omnidian Inc. related to labor compliance, workers' compensation, criminal background checks, and hazardous materials. The legal entities and relationships focus on statutory compliance certifications and the Contractor's obligations to the District and regulatory bodies such as the Department of Industrial Relations and Department of Justice. The clause text includes references to specific California Labor and Education Code sections, emphasizing the Contractor's legal duties before commencing work.

source_clause_title: 28.3. Labor Compliance:  Contractor shall perform the Work of the Project while complying with

entities.name: Contractor
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to perform the Work of the Project and comply with all applicable labor, anti-discrimination, and other legal requirements under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Contractor shall perform the Work of the Project while complying with all the applicable regulations...

entities.name: Contractor's subcontractor(s)
entities.legal_role: Entities engaged by the Contractor who must also comply with the same legal provisions as the Contractor under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Contractor agrees to require like compliance by all its subcontractor(s).

entities.name: District
entities.legal_role: The party receiving the Work and holding rights such as assignment of anti-trust claims and control over assignment of the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: District reserves the right to occupy the Sites...; Contractor shall not assign... without the prior written consent of the District.

entities.name: Department of Industrial Relations
entities.legal_role: The governmental body responsible for labor compliance monitoring and enforcement over the Contractor's performance.
entities.evidence_quote: subject to labor compliance monitoring and enforcement by the Department of Industrial Relations.

entities.name: California Fair Employment and Housing Act
entities.legal_role: A statutory framework that the Contractor and its subcontractors must comply with to prevent discrimination.
entities.evidence_quote: Contractor herein agrees to comply with the provisions of the California Fair Employment and Housing Act...

entities.name: Federal Civil Rights Act of 1964
entities.legal_role: Federal statute mandating non-discrimination compliance by the Contractor and subcontractors.
entities.evidence_quote: the Federal Civil Rights Act of 1964, as set forth in Public Law 88-352, and all amendments thereto

entities.name: Executive Order 11246
entities.legal_role: An executive mandate requiring non-discrimination compliance by the Contractor and subcontractors.
entities.evidence_quote: Executive Order 11246; and all administrative rules and regulations found to be applicable to Contractor and all of its subcontractors.

entities.name: Section 4 of the Clayton Act (15 U.S.C. Sec. 15)
entities.legal_role: A federal statutory cause of action for anti-trust claims that Contractor and subcontractors assign to the District.
entities.evidence_quote: all causes of action they may have under Section 4 of the Clayton Act (15 U.S.C. Sec. 15)...

entities.name: Cartwright Act (Chapter 2 of Part 2 of Division 7 of the Business and Professions Code)
entities.legal_role: A California statutory cause of action for anti-trust claims that Contractor and subcontractors assign to the District.
entities.evidence_quote: or under the Cartwright Act (Chapter 2 (commencing with Section 16700) of Part 2 of Division 7 of the Business and Professions Code)

entities.name: Final Payment
entities.legal_role: The event triggering the automatic assignment of anti-trust claims from Contractor and subcontractors to the District.
entities.evidence_quote: This assignment shall be made and become effective at the time the District tenders final payment to the Contractor

entities.name: Claims Presentation Requirements under Government Code Chapters 1 and 2 of Part 3 of Division 3.6 of Title 1
entities.legal_role: Procedural requirements that the Contractor must satisfy before bringing civil action against the District.
entities.evidence_quote: Contractor shall comply with all claims presentation requirements as provided in Chapter 1... and Chapter 2... as a condition precedent to the Contractor’s right to bring a civil action against the District.

entities.name: Tolling of claim presentation time during dispute resolution
entities.legal_role: Suspends the running of the time to present claims while the claim is under dispute and during meet and confer processes.
entities.evidence_quote: the running of the time within which a claim must be presented to the District shall be tolled from the time the Contractor submits its written claim until the time the claim is denied

entities.name: Obligation to continue Work during dispute
entities.legal_role: Requires Contractor and subcontractors to continue performing Work without delay during disputes unless District agrees in writing.
entities.evidence_quote: Pending resolution of the dispute, Contractor and its subcontractors shall continue to perform the Work... and shall not cause a delay of the Work during any dispute... except by written agreement of the District.

entities.name: Each party bears its own litigation costs
entities.legal_role: Specifies that each party is responsible for its own litigation and related expenses if enforcement of the Agreement is necessary.
entities.evidence_quote: each party shall bear its own litigation and collection expenses, witness fees, court costs, and attorney’s fees.

entities.name: Restriction on Contractor's assignment rights
entities.legal_role: Prohibits Contractor from assigning or transferring rights or obligations under the Agreement without District's prior written consent.
entities.evidence_quote: Contractor shall not assign or transfer in any way any or all of its rights, burdens, duties, or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the District.

entities.entity_type: Time is of the Essence Clause
entities.name: Time is of the essence
entities.legal_role: Establishes that timely performance and compliance with Agreement provisions are essential and material terms.
entities.evidence_quote: Time is of the essence in the performance of and compliance with each of the provisions and conditions of this Agreement.

entities.name: Governing law and venue
entities.legal_role: Specifies that the Agreement is governed by California law and venue for disputes is in the County of the District's administration office.
entities.evidence_quote: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California with venue of any action in a County in which the District administration office is located.

entities.name: Binding upon Parties and their successors and assigns
entities.legal_role: Makes the Agreement binding on the Parties and their successors and assigns, ensuring continuity of obligations and benefits.
entities.evidence_quote: This Agreement shall be binding upon the Parties and upon their successors and assigns and shall inure to the benefit of said Parties and their successors and assigns.

entities.name: District waiver limitations
entities.legal_role: Clarifies that waiver of one term or breach by the District does not waive other terms or breaches.
entities.evidence_quote: District's waiver of any term... shall not constitute the waiver of any other term... or the waiver of a breach of any other term...

entities.name: Invalid term severability
entities.legal_role: Provides that if any provision is found illegal or unenforceable, the remainder of the Agreement remains valid and enforceable.
entities.evidence_quote: If any provision of this Agreement is declared... to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable... the remaining parts... shall not be affected thereby...

entities.name: Entire contract integration
entities.legal_role: Declares that this Agreement supersedes all prior agreements and constitutes the full understanding between the Parties.
entities.evidence_quote: This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement between the Parties and fully supersedes any and all prior agreements...

entities.name: No oral modifications
entities.legal_role: Prevents any oral agreements or conversations from modifying the written terms of the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: No oral agreement or conversation... shall affect or modify any of the terms or obligations contained in any of the documents comprising the Agreement.

relationships.legal_significance: District's waiver of any term or breach does not waive other terms or breaches, preserving District's rights under the Agreement.

extractor_notes: This clause contains multiple distinct legal provisions beyond labor compliance, including anti-discrimination, anti-trust claim assignment, dispute resolution, litigation cost allocation, assignment restrictions, and standard contract boilerplate clauses such as governing law, severability, entire agreement, and no oral modification. The extraction treats each separately as entities and relationships to capture the full legal framework explicitly stated.

entities.name: O&M Contractor

entities.evidence_quote: Inform Customer and warranty providers of all deficiencies identified. Provide Customer with recommendations for corrective action.

entities.legal_role: Equipment subject to inspection for damage, discoloration, or delamination as part of preventative maintenance.
entities.evidence_quote: Inspect photovoltaic (PV) modules for damage, discoloration or delamination.

entities.name: mounting system
entities.legal_role: Structural component subject to inspection for damage or corrosion.
entities.evidence_quote: Inspect mounting system for damage or corrosion.

entities.name: Inverter
entities.legal_role: Electrical equipment subject to cleaning, inspection, tightening, and other preventive maintenance per OEM warranty.
entities.evidence_quote: Clean all filters and fans. Inspect inverter pad and container. Tighten wire terminations inside inverter. All other preventive maintenance required by original equipment manufacturer (OEM) warranty.

entities.name: Electrical Balance of System (BOS)
entities.legal_role: Electrical components subject to inspection, thermo-graphic analysis, testing, and repair.
entities.evidence_quote: Inspect ground braids, electrodes and conductors for damage. Perform thermo-graphic analysis of combiner boxes, inverters, transformers, and conductor connections to buses, breakers or disconnects. Test and record all circuits, open circuit voltage and short circuit current and repair any fault circuits. Inspection of all terminal connections for torque.

entities.name: Meteorological Station
entities.legal_role: Optional equipment subject to inspection and cleaning if present at the site.
entities.evidence_quote: Meteorological Station (if present) Inspect weather measurement equipment for damage. Clean pyranometers and reference cells.

entities.name: Site Conditions
entities.legal_role: Physical site elements subject to inspection for drainage, vegetation hazards, safety conditions, and signage.
entities.evidence_quote: Inspect drainage conditions. Inspect vegetation for array shading or fire hazards, and remove any obstructions causing shade or hazard issues, including ongoing tree trimming as needed. Inspect safety conditions and proper signage.

entities.name: Maintenance Reporting
entities.legal_role: Obligation to record inspection results, document damage, notify Customer and warranty providers of deficiencies, and provide corrective recommendations.
entities.evidence_quote: Record results of all inspections. Take photographs of any damage or defects identified. Inform Customer and warranty providers of all deficiencies identified. Provide Customer with recommendations for corrective action.

entities.name: Sensor Calibration
entities.legal_role: Obligation to calibrate pyranometers every 24 months, perform field comparisons, adjust sensors, and optionally replace sensors.
entities.evidence_quote: Every twenty-four (24) months, O&M Contractor will have the pyranometer calibrated by the manufacturer of each sensor. Field comparison of pyranometers and reference cells to calibrated sensor. Adjust field sensor to within ±3% of calibrated sensor and record changed parameters. O&M Contractor may replace pyranometers in lieu of field calibration.

entities.name: OEM warranty
entities.legal_role: Warranty under which preventive maintenance requirements for the inverter must be performed.
entities.evidence_quote: All other preventive maintenance required by original equipment manufacturer (OEM) warranty.

relationships.legal_significance: The O&M Contractor is legally obligated to inspect the photovoltaic modules for damage, discoloration, or delamination as part of preventative maintenance.
relationships.evidence_quote: Inspect photovoltaic (PV) modules for damage, discoloration or delamination.

relationships.legal_significance: The O&M Contractor must inspect the mounting system for damage or corrosion, ensuring structural integrity is maintained.
relationships.evidence_quote: Inspect mounting system for damage or corrosion.

relationships.legal_significance: The O&M Contractor is required to perform cleaning, inspection, tightening, and other preventive maintenance on the inverter as mandated by the OEM warranty.
relationships.evidence_quote: Clean all filters and fans. Inspect inverter pad and container. Tighten wire terminations inside inverter. All other preventive maintenance required by original equipment manufacturer (OEM) warranty.

relationships.legal_significance: The O&M Contractor must inspect, analyze, test, and repair the Electrical Balance of System components to ensure proper operation and safety.
relationships.evidence_quote: Inspect ground braids, electrodes and conductors for damage. Perform thermo-graphic analysis of combiner boxes, inverters, transformers, and conductor connections to buses, breakers or disconnects. Test and record all circuits, open circuit voltage and short circuit current and repair any fault circuits. Inspection of all terminal connections for torque.

relationships.legal_significance: If a meteorological station is present, the O&M Contractor must inspect and clean its equipment, ensuring accurate weather data collection.
relationships.evidence_quote: Meteorological Station (if present) Inspect weather measurement equipment for damage. Clean pyranometers and reference cells.

relationships.legal_significance: The O&M Contractor must inspect site conditions including drainage, vegetation hazards, and safety signage, and remove obstructions to maintain safe and efficient operation.
relationships.evidence_quote: Inspect drainage conditions. Inspect vegetation for array shading or fire hazards, and remove any obstructions causing shade or hazard issues, including ongoing tree trimming as needed. Inspect safety conditions and proper signage.

relationships.legal_significance: The O&M Contractor must document inspection results, photograph defects, notify the Customer and warranty providers of deficiencies, and provide corrective recommendations, ensuring transparency and accountability.
relationships.evidence_quote: Record results of all inspections. Take photographs of any damage or defects identified. Inform Customer and warranty providers of all deficiencies identified. Provide Customer with recommendations for corrective action.

relationships.legal_significance: The O&M Contractor is obligated to calibrate pyranometer sensors every 24 months or replace them, ensuring measurement accuracy as required by the agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: Every twenty-four (24) months, O&M Contractor will have the pyranometer calibrated by the manufacturer of each sensor. Field comparison of pyranometers and reference cells to calibrated sensor. Adjust field sensor to within ±3% of calibrated sensor and record changed parameters. O&M Contractor may replace pyranometers in lieu of field calibration.

relationships.legal_significance: Preventive maintenance on the inverter must comply with the requirements set forth by the OEM warranty, linking maintenance obligations to warranty conditions.
relationships.evidence_quote: All other preventive maintenance required by original equipment manufacturer (OEM) warranty.

relationships.legal_significance: The O&M Contractor must inform the Customer of all deficiencies and provide recommendations, enabling the Customer to take corrective action and enforce warranty rights.
relationships.evidence_quote: Inform Customer and warranty providers of all deficiencies identified. Provide Customer with recommendations for corrective action.

extractor_notes: This clause details the specific preventative maintenance, inspection, testing, and reporting obligations of the O&M Contractor, including sensor calibration frequency and conditions. The clause explicitly ties inverter maintenance to OEM warranty requirements and establishes notification duties to the Customer and warranty providers. The clause is labeled as 'termination' type but contains no explicit termination rights or conditions.

source_clause_title: 2.1. This Agreement shall become effective on the Effective Date and shall remain

relationships.legal_significance: The defined 'Term' includes the renewal term as one of its constituent periods.
relationships.evidence_quote: The initial term, renewal term, and any additional term thereafter are collectively referenced as “Term.”

relationships.legal_significance: The defined 'Term' includes any additional terms exercised after the renewal term.
relationships.evidence_quote: The initial term, renewal term, and any additional term thereafter are collectively referenced as “Term.”

extractor_notes: The clause defines a multi-stage contract term structure with an initial fixed term, an automatic renewal term subject to District's termination right, and multiple additional optional terms exercisable at District's sole discretion. The District's rights to terminate and accept additional terms are explicitly stated, emphasizing its control over contract continuation.

extractor_notes: The clause text is partial and appears to be a continuation; the key legal entities and relationships relate to the District's right to retain payments pending indemnification and resolution of claims, and the survival of indemnification obligations beyond contract termination.

entities.entity_type: Measurement Device for PV System Production
entities.name: PV production meter
entities.legal_role: This device is legally significant as it provides the official measurement of the photovoltaic system's electricity production, which is essential for performance monitoring and contractual compliance.
entities.evidence_quote: Separate PV production and consumption meters to measure PV system production and on-site consumption of electricity (separate from net consumption).

entities.entity_type: Measurement Device for On-Site Electricity Consumption
entities.name: Consumption meter
entities.legal_role: This device is legally significant as it measures the on-site electricity consumption separately from net consumption, enabling accurate monitoring and reporting under the contract.
entities.evidence_quote: Separate PV production and consumption meters to measure PV system production and on-site consumption of electricity (separate from net consumption).

entities.name: Customer website
entities.legal_role: This website is legally significant as it provides the Customer with updated operational performance data every 15 minutes, fulfilling the Contractor's obligation to provide timely performance information.
entities.evidence_quote: Customer website updated every 15 minutes with operational performance from the beginning of operation.

entities.entity_type: Data Transmission Infrastructure Maintained by Contractor
entities.name: Cellular data connection or other data connection
entities.legal_role: This data connection is legally significant because it ensures the Contractor's responsibility to maintain independent data transmission infrastructure, excluding use of the Customer’s internet connection.
entities.evidence_quote: Cellular data connection or other data connection maintained by the Contractor. System may not use Customer’s internet connection.

entities.name: Internet-based monitoring and reporting portal
entities.legal_role: This portal is legally significant as it grants the Customer full data access, including weather parameters, fulfilling transparency and data access obligations under the contract.

entities.entity_type: Time Synchronization Standard for Site Meters

entities.legal_role: This synchronization requirement is legally significant as it ensures data consistency and comparability across site meters and utility meters, which is critical for accurate performance measurement.
entities.evidence_quote: Synchronized clock intervals for all site meters, including production and consumption meter. Clock intervals shall be synchronized with utility meters where feasible.

entities.name: Microsoft Excel format
entities.legal_role: This requirement is legally significant as it mandates that all site data must be downloadable in a widely accessible format, ensuring the Customer’s ability to analyze and use the data.
entities.evidence_quote: All site data must be able to be downloaded to Customer’s computer in Microsoft Excel format.

entities.name: Login credentials
entities.legal_role: These credentials are legally significant as they provide the Customer with authorized access to the monitoring system during the term of the O&M Agreement.

entities.evidence_quote: All fees for monitoring software and cellular/data fees included under this scope.

entities.name: Contractor

entities.name: Customer

relationships.legal_significance: Granting the Customer full data access, including weather parameters, through the portal ensures transparency and enables the Customer to monitor system performance effectively.

relationships.legal_significance: Requiring data to be downloadable in Microsoft Excel format ensures the Customer can use and analyze the data conveniently, supporting their operational and reporting needs.
relationships.evidence_quote: All site data must be able to be downloaded to Customer’s computer in Microsoft Excel format.

source_clause_title: 23.1.2. Workers’ Compensation and Employers’ Liability Insurance .  Workers’

source_clause_title: 2. Renovation, Repair and Painting Rule, Section 402(c)(3) of the Toxic

entities.name: EPA
entities.legal_role: The EPA is the federal agency that imposes regulatory requirements for lead safe work practices under the Renovation, Repair and Painting Rule.
entities.evidence_quote: The EPA requires lead safe work practices to reduce exposure to lead hazards created by renovation, repair and painting activities that disturb lead -based paint.

entities.name: Renovation, Repair and Painting Rule (RRP)
entities.legal_role: The RRP is a federal regulatory rule mandating certified lead-safe work practices for renovations disturbing lead-based paint in certain buildings.
entities.evidence_quote: Pursuant to the Renovation, Repair and Painting Rule (RRP), renovations in homes, childcare facilities, and schools built prior to 1978 must be conducted by certified renovations firms...

entities.name: certified renovations firms
entities.legal_role: Certified renovations firms are legally authorized entities required to conduct renovations disturbing lead-based paint under the RRP.
entities.evidence_quote: renovations in homes, childcare facilities, and schools built prior to 1978 must be conducted by certified renovations firms

entities.name: renovators with training by a EPA-accredited training provider
entities.legal_role: Renovators trained by EPA-accredited providers are individuals legally required to perform renovation work under the RRP.
entities.evidence_quote: using renovators with training by a EPA- accredited training provider

entities.name: title 40 of the Code of Federal Regulations as part 745 (40 CFR 745)
entities.legal_role: 40 CFR 745 contains the federal regulations governing lead-based materials compliance relevant to the RRP.
entities.evidence_quote: including those rules and regulations appearing within title 40 of the Code of Federal Regulations as part 745 (40 CFR 745)

entities.name: contractors who disturb lead-based paint

entities.evidence_quote: The RRP requirements apply to all contractors who disturb lead- based paint in a six -square-foot or greater area indoors or a 20 -square-foot or greater area outdoors.

entities.name: DPH-certified inspector or risk assessor

entities.evidence_quote: If a DPH-certified inspector or risk assessor determines that a home constructed before 1978 is lead-free, the federal certification is not required

entities.name: homes, childcare facilities, and schools built prior to 1978

entities.evidence_quote: renovations in homes, childcare facilities, and schools built prior to 1978 must be conducted by certified renovations firms

relationships.legal_significance: The EPA issues the Renovation, Repair and Painting Rule, establishing the regulatory framework for lead-safe renovation practices.
relationships.evidence_quote: The EPA requires lead safe work practices... Pursuant to the Renovation, Repair and Painting Rule (RRP)

relationships.legal_significance: The RRP mandates that renovations disturbing lead-based paint in covered buildings must be performed by certified renovations firms.
relationships.evidence_quote: renovations... must be conducted by certified renovations firms

relationships.legal_significance: The RRP requires renovators to have training from EPA-accredited providers to ensure compliance with lead-safe work practices.
relationships.evidence_quote: using renovators with training by a EPA- accredited training provider

relationships.legal_significance: The RRP incorporates compliance with lead-based material regulations found in 40 CFR 745 as part of its legal requirements.
relationships.evidence_quote: including those rules and regulations appearing within title 40 of the Code of Federal Regulations as part 745 (40 CFR 745)

relationships.legal_significance: The RRP applies to contractors disturbing lead-based paint in specified minimum areas, defining the scope of regulated activities.
relationships.evidence_quote: The RRP requirements apply to all contractors who disturb lead- based paint in a six -square-foot or greater area indoors or a 20 -square-foot or greater area outdoors.

relationships.legal_significance: DPH-certified inspectors or risk assessors can legally determine that a pre-1978 building is lead-free, exempting it from federal certification requirements.
relationships.evidence_quote: If a DPH-certified inspector or risk assessor determines that a home constructed before 1978 is lead-free, the federal certification is not required

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly defines the regulatory framework and certification requirements under the EPA's Renovation, Repair and Painting Rule, including the scope of application and exceptions based on lead-free determinations by DPH-certified inspectors or risk assessors.

source_clause_title: 2.3. District may, at any time, with our without reason, terminate this Agreement

entities.name: District
entities.legal_role: The party with the unilateral right to terminate the Agreement at any time with or without reason, triggering contractual consequences.
entities.evidence_quote: District may, at any time, with our without reason, terminate this Agreement

entities.name: Contractor
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to perform Services under the Agreement and entitled to compensation for Services satisfactorily rendered up to termination.
entities.evidence_quote: compensate Contractor only for Services satisfactorily rendered to the date of termination

entities.name: Thirty (30) days’ notice
entities.legal_role: The minimum advance written notice period the District must provide before termination becomes effective.
entities.evidence_quote: with thirty (30) days’ notice

entities.name: Written notice by District
entities.legal_role: The formal communication method by which the District effectuates termination and stops further performance by the Contractor.
entities.evidence_quote: Written notice by District shall be sufficient to stop further performance of Services by Contractor

entities.name: Notice deemed given
entities.legal_role: The legal rule establishing when the termination notice is considered received by the Contractor, affecting timing of termination effects.
entities.evidence_quote: Notice shall be deemed given when received by the Contractor or no later than three (3) calendar days after the date of mailing, whichever is sooner

relationships.legal_significance: Identifies the District as the party authorized to terminate the Agreement under the specified clause.
relationships.evidence_quote: District may, at any time, with our without reason, terminate this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: The District is obligated to provide written notice to effectuate termination and stop further performance.
relationships.evidence_quote: Written notice by District shall be sufficient to stop further performance of Services by Contractor

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor is the recipient of the written termination notice, triggering cessation of services.
relationships.evidence_quote: Written notice by District shall be sufficient to stop further performance of Services by Contractor

relationships.legal_significance: The District must provide the Contractor with at least thirty (30) days’ notice before termination is effective.
relationships.evidence_quote: with thirty (30) days’ notice

relationships.legal_significance: Defines the timing for when the written notice is legally considered received by the Contractor, affecting termination timing.
relationships.evidence_quote: Notice shall be deemed given when received by the Contractor or no later than three (3) calendar days after the date of mailing, whichever is sooner

relationships.legal_significance: The District must compensate the Contractor only for Services satisfactorily rendered up to the termination date.
relationships.evidence_quote: compensate Contractor only for Services satisfactorily rendered to the date of termination

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly grants the District an unrestricted termination right with a 30-day notice period and specifies the method and timing of notice delivery, as well as the Contractor's entitlement to payment only for satisfactorily rendered services up to termination.

source_clause_title: 3.4. Any charges for Extra Services shall be paid by the District only to the extent

entities.name: District

entities.evidence_quote: Any charges for Extra Services shall be paid by the District only to the extent that the claimed Extra Services were authorized as indicated herein and that the Extra Services have been satisfactorily completed to the District’s approval.

entities.name: Contractor
entities.legal_role: The party performing Extra Services and seeking payment from the District, subject to prior written authorization.
entities.evidence_quote: If any service is performed by Contractor without prior written authorization by District, District will not be obligated to pay for such service.

entities.name: Extra Services

relationships.legal_significance: The District is obligated to pay the Contractor only for Extra Services that have been authorized and satisfactorily completed, protecting the District from unauthorized charges.
relationships.evidence_quote: If any service is performed by Contractor without prior written authorization by District, District will not be obligated to pay for such service.

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly conditions payment on prior written authorization and satisfactory completion approved by the District, establishing clear payment triggers and limiting the District's financial exposure for unauthorized services.

source_clause_title: 21.1. To the furthest extent permitted by California law, Contractor shall indemnify and hold

entities.name: Contractor
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to indemnify and hold harmless the Indemnified Parties from claims arising from the Agreement's performance.
entities.evidence_quote: Contractor shall indemnify and hold harmless the District, its agents, representatives, officers, consultants, employees, and volunteers

entities.name: District, its agents, representatives, officers, consultants, employees, and volunteers
entities.legal_role: The parties protected by the indemnity obligation against claims arising from the Agreement's performance.
entities.evidence_quote: the District, its agents, representatives, officers, consultants, employees, and volunteers (the “Indemnified Parties”)

entities.name: any and all demands, losses, liabilities, claims, suits, and actions
entities.legal_role: The types of legal or financial claims from which the Indemnified Parties are protected under the indemnity obligation.
entities.evidence_quote: any and all demands, losses, liabilities, claims, suits, and actions (the “claims”) of any kind, nature, and description, including, but not limited to, attorneys’ fees and costs

entities.name: active negligence or willful misconduct of the Indemnified Parties
entities.legal_role: The condition under which the indemnity obligation does not apply, excluding claims caused by the Indemnified Parties' own active negligence or willful misconduct.
entities.evidence_quote: unless the claims are caused by the active negligence or willful misconduct of the Indemnified Parties

entities.name: California law
entities.legal_role: The legal jurisdiction limiting the extent of the indemnity obligation to the furthest extent permitted by its laws.
entities.evidence_quote: To the furthest extent permitted by California law

relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor is legally obligated to indemnify and hold harmless the District and related parties from claims arising from the Agreement's performance, protecting them from liability.
relationships.evidence_quote: Contractor shall indemnify and hold harmless the District, its agents, representatives, officers, consultants, employees, and volunteers

relationships.legal_significance: The indemnity covers any and all claims, including attorneys’ fees and costs, directly or indirectly arising from the Agreement's performance, defining the breadth of Contractor's liability.
relationships.evidence_quote: from any and all demands, losses, liabilities, claims, suits, and actions (the “claims”) of any kind, nature, and description, including, but not limited to, attorneys’ fees and costs, directly or indirectly arising from, arising out of, connected with, or resulting from the performance of this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: The indemnity obligation excludes claims caused by the active negligence or willful misconduct of the Indemnified Parties, limiting Contractor's indemnity responsibility.
relationships.evidence_quote: unless the claims are caused by the active negligence or willful misconduct of the Indemnified Parties

relationships.legal_significance: The indemnity obligation is limited to the furthest extent permitted by California law, ensuring compliance with applicable legal standards.
relationships.evidence_quote: To the furthest extent permitted by California law

extractor_notes: The clause establishes a broad indemnity obligation by the Contractor to protect the District and related parties from claims arising from the Agreement, subject to exclusions for the District's own active negligence or willful misconduct and limited by California law.
